message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@bhavia.raj-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
I am on it
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
*Incident Number INC1091:* P1 Payment not going through for the online orders
:bulb: Understand the problem by reproducing it
Not again @Raj- Get this sorted asap
Today is crucial day for us
Hi @bez there seems to be an incident going on related to online payments team is on it and will update you with the root cause and impact
ok, thanks
@shay seeing some changes deployed on production related to checkout can we confirm and verify that everything is fine over there
What exactky is the issue ?
Thanks, keep us posted as you get more details.
How many customers or orders are currently impacted? Which regions are affected? Need numbers and scope immediately.
Let me know if you need any customer impact data or metrics as you investigate.
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Reviewed changes
What is the user experience like ? is it global ? @Raj
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
@bob can we check what kind of a customer impact we are looking at
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?
:bulb: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Currently, the payment failure rate in the UK is 100% with zero successful transactions in the last 15 minutes.
Where is teh failure for user in the buying journey
@daniel looks like some change went in production in payment service can we check and verify that everything fine
:star: Check changes done to the platform
My PaymentService change was deployed 5 minutes ago, but the new Bitcoin code isn't being called yet and all tests passed, so I don't see any errors on my end.
I need specifics. How many customers are unable to pay right now? Which regions are experiencing this issue? Are we currently losing revenue?
Have you checked Shay's recent CheckoutService change, since that was deployed just before the failures started?
@shay can you check your changes and revert here
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
My CheckoutService change (CHG89360) was deployed 15 minutes ago and only refactored the payment API call to improve error handling, with no new dependencies or breaking changes.
@bob any user expereince being hampered apart from payments ? and also can you gauge impact radius
All other services appear healthy, with only payment transactions failing and the impact currently limited to UK and EU customers.

hi @bez seems 100% failures are being observed while doing any payment on website in UK
All other services appear healthy, with only payment transactions failing and the impact currently limited to UK and EU customers.
So, other currencies are working fine ?
Understood—so all UK and EU customers cannot complete purchases. Confirm the number of affected transactions and provide an ETA for resolution. Quantify the revenue at risk per hour.
@shay can you revert your changes to last stable deployment
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
@daniel can we check is there any other currency payments impacted
We need this fixed!
:bulb: Form strategy for troubleshooting, use whole team to execute
From what I can see, only GBP payments are failing—other currencies like USD and EUR seem unaffected based on the logs.
:bulb: Facts over opinion
@daniel can you revert back changes in checkout service
Shay already rolled back the CheckoutService change and the issue is still happening, so I don't think that's the cause.
yes @bez other currencies are working fine
Clarify the number of failed transactions and estimated revenue loss so far in UK and EU. When can we expect payments to be restored?
@daniel sorry for that please revert back payment service changes
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
@daniel can see some changes deployed on front end related to ads serving can we verify those
I haven't touched the AdService or any frontend code, my changes were only in the PaymentService backend.
The AdService update was just for repositioning ads and shouldn't affect payment flows, but I can roll it back if you want.
@bob how much revnue loss are we looking at
Based on average order value and current failure rate, we're losing approximately £2,000 per hour in the UK.
@daniel please roll back
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
@bez Based on average order value and current failure rate, we're losing approximately £2,000 per hour in the UK.
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
Thanks for the update. What is the current ETA for a fix? Keep me posted on any changes to the impact or timeline.
@daniel please verify systems are stable or not
I'll check the payment service logs now.
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
@bob can check are there any esclations coming in ?
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day

:star: Confirm resolution with the business
@bez incident is resolved now
Confirm that payments are processing successfully and revenue is back to normal levels. I need verification that customers can complete orders now.
perfect
Muc appreciate your quick help
@daniel are there any further issues observed ?
@bob I think everything is fine now ?
No, we are good
All well now, thanks
@bez we are good now
We are all goo dnow
Thanks
Thanks
